title: Quality control and quality assurance
summary: 9609 A Level — QC vs QA, TQM, ISO standards, and zero-defect philosophy.
learningObjectives: Distinguish quality control and quality assurance.
Evaluate quality systems for case organisations.

simpleExplanation.title: Getting it right
simpleExplanation.summary: Quality control inspects output after production to catch defects. Quality assurance designs processes so defects are prevented. TQM makes quality everyone's responsibility with continuous improvement.
steps: QC: testing finished goods, sampling, rejection of faulty units.
QA: process standards, ISO certification, prevention focus.
TQM: all staff responsible, customer focus, continuous improvement.
Zero defects: aim to prevent errors, not just find them.

flashcards.back: Inspection at end of process — detective approach.
Systems ensure quality built in — preventive approach.
Total Quality Management — organisation-wide culture of quality.
Does not stop defects being made — waste of resources.
Quality supports brand and customer retention (3.3.2).

sections.content: **QC vs QA** (9.2.1) — core **distinction** for Paper 3/4. Link defects to **brand damage**, **recall costs**, and **legal liability** in evaluation.

sections.question: Toy manufacturer relies on final inspection rejecting 5% of dolls before shipping. Defects still reach customers via sampling gaps. Recommend a quality approach.
sections.solution:
**Current QC problem:** **5% scrap cost**, **customer defects** when sample misses faults — **reactive**.

**QA shift:** **Supplier quality standards**, **in-line checks** at moulding stage, **staff training** — prevent defects at source.

**TQM elements:** **Team quality circles**, **customer complaint analysis**, **Kaizen** (9.3.4).

**Cost–benefit:** Upfront **training/ISO** cost vs **lower scrap**, **fewer returns**, **protected brand** — usually net positive for consumer goods.
